blocks.text: Perfect. Now let me run the DESeq2 analysis in R:

input.code:
# Step 1: Load raw counts and metadata
library(DESeq2)

# Read data
counts_df <- read.csv('counts_de.csv', row.names=1)
metadata_df <- read.csv('metadata_de.csv', row.names=1)

# Ensure counts are integer
counts_matrix <- as.matrix(counts_df)
mode(counts_matrix) <- 'integer'

# Create DESeqDataSet
dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = counts_matrix,
  colData = metadata_df,
  design = ~ cell_type
)

print(paste("Loaded DESeqDataSet:"))
print(paste("  Samples:", ncol(dds)))
print(paste("  Genes:", nrow(dds)))
print(paste("  Cell type levels:", paste(unique(dds$cell_type), collapse=', ')))

# Check sample counts
print("Sample counts per cell type:")
print(table(dds$cell_type))
